title: From relativistic quantum mechanics to quantum relativity
abstract: Substituting the energy operator and momentum operator under the space-time coordinate representation into the second-order energy and momentum equation of special relativity can obtain the Klein-Gordon equation, and substituting them into the first-order energy and momentum, equation can obtain the Dirac equation; conversely, substituting the time operator and space operator under the energy and momentum representation into the second-order space-time relationship of special relativity can obtain the quantum relativistic space-time relationship equation of the second-order differential form of energy and momentum, and substituting them into the first-order space-time relationship can obtain the quantum relativistic space-time relationship equation of the first-order differential form of energy and momentum. This method can also be applied to general relativity, thereby obtaining the corresponding quantum gravitational relativistic differential equation.
